other_observers: Nick Komar, Nick A. Komar, and Andrew Spencer.; We were the finders of the birds. I don't remember who was the first to ID the first one.
specific_location: The Sewage Ponds at the South end of Dove Creek.
description: The wing tips were rounded, not as pointed as a Common. The white in the wing tips was a slightly different shape than Common. Buffy spots on the upper and under wings and buff on the underside of the body.Behaviors: They were with Common Nighthawks flying over some sewage ponds, good comparison.Call: Didn't hear it call or sing.Plumage: Adult
